bulletsEn: Major open-weight model releases included Arcee Trinity-Large-Thinking, Z.ai GLM-5V-Turbo, and Falcon Perception, emphasizing developer utility, efficiency, and multimodal capabilities. These models, often released under open licenses, garnered significant community interest for their competitive performance in reasoning, vision coding, and segmentation tasks. The launches signal a growing trend towards

The Claude Code source leak became the top story, revealing substantial source artifacts via shipped source maps and package contents, triggering rapid public reverse-engineering and mirroring. This incident quickly shifted discussion from an "embarrassing leak" to what it revealed about state-of-the-art agent harness design, highlighting the unexpected exposure of proprietary information. The exposed material did

Anthropic's Claude Code now features computer use, enabling agents to interact with UIs, open apps, and test directly from the CLI in a research preview. This innovation allows for closed-loop verification, where agents can build, run, inspect, fix, and re-test code, addressing a critical missing piece for reliable application iteration. Anthropic is reportedly introducing a new, more intelligent "Capybara" tier above Opus, demonstrating significant performance gains in coding and reasoning. This leak highlights Anthropic's aggressive scaling ambitions, with speculation around a ~10T parameter model and Google's potential funding for their data center, though this intense scaling is causing visible infrastructure strain and user complaints

Google launched Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a new realtime model for voice and vision agents, emphasizing lower latency, improved function calling, better noisy-environment robustness, and 2x longer conversation memory. This model is rolling out across Gemini Live, Search Live, AI Studio preview, and enterprise CX surfaces, supporting 70 languages and 128k

ARC-AGI-3 was launched as a new benchmark for "general" agentic reasoning, designed to measure a system's ability to approach new tasks with human-like learning efficiency without prior exposure or intervention. items.title: MiniMax 2.7: GLM-5 at 1/3 cost SOTA Open Model

bulletsEn: The OmniCoder-9B and Qwen 3.5 9B models are gaining attention for their impressive performance, often compared to much larger models despite their smaller size. These models demonstrate significant potential for local deployment on consumer hardware, offering an alternative to cloud-based solutions with increasing quota restrictions.

Anthropic launched interactive charts and diagrams in chat, demonstrating a clear move towards richer frontends for LLMs beyond simple text. This innovation allows for more dynamic and visual interactions, enhancing the overall user experience and utility of AI models. It signifies a broader trend in AI development focusing on multimodal outputs and interactive interfaces.
